codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of deterministic board generation. The DJB2 hash + mulberry32 PRNG is well-implemented with proper Unicode support via codePointAt. The items pool has exactly 45 unique strings. The board generation algorithm correctly filters the center item, shuffles the remaining 44, takes 24, and inserts the center at index 12. The game route uses Zod-validated search params and redirects to a random seed when none is provided. All edge cases (empty seed, XSS payloads, Unicode, special characters, very long strings) are handled correctly. React's JSX escaping provides natural XSS protection for the seed display. Tests are comprehensive with 32 board-generation specific tests covering determinism, edge cases, composition, and pool validation.

issues.description: The 'URL-encoded seeds produce same board as decoded' test only verifies that generateBoard('abc def') === generateBoard('abc def') — it doesn't actually test URL encoding/decoding round-trip behavior. The comment acknowledges URL decoding happens at the router level, so this is a documentation-focused gap rather than a functional bug, but the test name is slightly misleading.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The SKILL.md references 'app/' paths in examples (e.g., 'app/lib/bingo.test.ts', 'app/components/Board.tsx') which don't match the actual 'src/' directory convention. The directory-convention.md library doc addresses this mismatch, but the SKILL.md itself still contains the outdated paths. This caused no issues for this worker but could confuse future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md example handoff references 'app/lib/bingo.ts', 'app/components/Board.tsx'. The directory-convention.md notes: 'AGENTS.md and the frontend-worker skill contain example paths referencing app/ — these are aspirational and do not match the actual project structure.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker had to check the Zod version (v4.3.6) during implementation. Zod v4 has API differences from v3 (e.g., different error handling, different optional/default chaining). This version-specific knowledge isn't documented in the library or AGENTS.md and could save future workers time if recorded.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker running: cat node_modules/zod/package.json | grep '"version"' to discover zod v4.3.6 before implementing validateSearch with Zod.

summary: The board-generation feature is well-implemented and passes review. The DJB2 hash + mulberry32 PRNG produces deterministic boards with correct center square placement. The 45-item pool is clean with no duplicates. Edge cases are thoroughly handled both in code and tests. XSS protection is inherent from React's JSX escaping. The only minor note is a weakly-named URL-encoding test that doesn't actually test URL round-tripping. All 125 tests pass (67 in bingo.test.ts alone), typecheck is clean, and the implementation matches the feature description and expected behavior requirements.
